competitions.product_info: Home – Rolex Day Date 18038 18k Solid Gold 1978 WIN A VINTAGE 18K SOLID GOLD ROLEX DAY-DATE 18038 W/ BOX Value: £8,000 Duration: 7 Days Age: Circa 1978 Max. Entries: 799 Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 23/06/26 The Rolex Day-Date is one of the most iconic watches of all time , and has been Rolex’s flagship model since its introduction in 1956, worn by presidents and influential figures across generations. This reference, the 18038 , dates to 1978 and is crafted in 18k solid yellow gold with the classic “champagne” dial . Featuring a sapphire crystal and the 3055 “single quick” calibre, this Day-Date is the perfect balance of vintage charm and everyday practicality . This watch is in very good condition , and is accompanied by its inner & outer box . 27.91% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 223 799 27.91% 576 Tickets remaining 576/799 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 4 Days 18 Hours 33 Minutes 55 Seconds ENTER NOW WIN A VINTAGE ROLEX DAY-DATE 18K SOLID GOLD 1978 ENTER NOW 27.91% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 223 799 27.91% 576 Tickets remaining 576/799 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 4 Days 18 Hours 33 Minutes 55 Seconds Value: £8,000 Duration: 7 Days Age: Circa 1978 Entries: 799 Max. Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 23/06/2026 The Rolex Day-Date is one of the most iconic watches of all time , and has been Rolex’s flagship model since its introduction in 1956, worn by presidents and influential figures across generations. This reference, the 18038 , dates to 1978 and is crafted in 18k solid yellow gold with the classic “champagne” dial . Featuring a sapphire crystal and the 3055 “single quick” calibre, this Day-Date is the perfect balance of vintage charm and everyday practicality . This watch is in very good condition , and is accompanied by its inner & outer box . #29 - The 18k Solid Gold Rolex Day Date 18038 w/ Box 1978 The History— The Example The Rolex Day-Date is one of the most iconic watches ever produced and has sat at the very top of the Rolex catalogue since its introduction in 1956. Crafted exclusively in precious metals and instantly recognisable by its day display at 12 o’clock, the Day-Date has long been associated with presidents, business leaders and influential figures across generations, earning it the enduring nickname of the “President.” This reference, the 18038 , dates to 1978 and represents one of the most desirable eras of Day-Date production. Crafted in 18k solid yellow gold and featuring the classic champagne dial , it captures everything collectors love about vintage Rolex. The 18038 was also an important step forward for the model, introducing a sapphire crystal and the Rolex calibre 3055 movement. Featuring the desirable single quickset date function, the calibre 3055 brought a level of practicality and convenience that earlier Day-Dates lacked, while maintaining the robustness and reliability for which Rolex is renowned. On the wrist, the watch delivers exactly what a Day-Date should: substantial without being excessive, elegant without feeling formal. The combination of yellow gold, fluted bezel and champagne dial remains one of the most timeless configurations Rolex has ever produced. This example is presented on an attractive brown leather strap, and is in very good condition , accompanied by its inner and outer box. Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 30/06/2026 The Rolex GMT-Master is one of the most iconic and popular watches ever made, and this example is a slight twist on a classic, known as the “Coke” for its red & black Coca-Cola colour palette bezel insert. This 16710 GMT-Master II reference is of its best iterations, perfectly balancing vintage styling with modern practicality . This example dates to 1997 and is in very sharp, immaculate condition , accompanied by its box, booklets & tag. #30 - The Rolex GMT-Master II "Coke" 16710 w/ Box, Booklets & Tag 1997 The History— The Example The Rolex GMT-Master II is one of the most iconic sports watches ever produced, originally developed for airline pilots travelling between time zones. This example is a slightly different take on the classic GMT formula, featuring the distinctive red and black “Coke” bezel – a configuration that has become increasingly sought after in recent years. The reference 16710 is widely regarded as one of the best GMT-Master II references, perfectly balancing vintage character with modern practicality. Powered by the Rolex calibre 3185 , it features an independently adjustable hour hand, allowing the wearer to track multiple time zones with ease. Dating to 1997 , this example is presented in exceptionally sharp, immaculate condition , with excellent case definition throughout. It is accompanied by its box, booklets and tag . A perfect GMT-Master II in one of the model’s most attractive configurations – versatile, iconic and increasingly difficult to find in such outstanding condition. competitions.product: Welcome to
competitions.product_info: Welcome to The Time Vault Club Where passion for horology meets opportunity. We exist to bring the world of exceptional watchmaking closer to you, from iconic Swiss craftsmanship to bespoke British design. Through our curated competitions, we give you the chance to discover and own watches most people only dream of. LIVE COMPETITION All of our prize draws are hosted live, so you can witness every moment as dream watches find new owners. With regular competitions featuring everything from iconic Swiss classics to standout modern releases, the next opportunity is always closer than you think. The watch comes with its original box, papers, booklets, tag and more, and was originally sold in Hong Kong. #31 - The Rolex Explorer II "Polar" 16570 w/ Original Box & Papers Set 2001 The History— The Example The Rolex Explorer II evolved from the earlier Explorer models by introducing a fixed 24-hour bezel and additional GMT hand, transforming it from a simple time-only tool watch into a purpose-built companion for explorers working in environments where distinguishing day from night could prove difficult. The reference 16570 is widely regarded as the sweet spot of the Explorer II lineage, combining classic proportions with the reliable Rolex calibre 3185 movement and independently adjustable GMT hand. Dating to 2001 , this “Polar” example features the model’s iconic white dial, offering superb legibility and a distinctive look that has become increasingly popular with collectors. Presented in excellent, sharp condition , the watch has been exceptionally well preserved and is accompanied by its original box, papers, booklets, tag and accessories . Originally supplied in Hong Kong , it is a superb, complete example of one of Rolex’s most wearable and increasingly collectible sports watches. Through our curated competitions, we give you the chance to discover and own watches most people only dream of. competitions.product: WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX EXPLORER II "POLAR" 16570 W/ BOX & PAPERS SET
competitions.product_info: Home – Rolex Explorer 16570 “Polar” WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX EXPLORER II "POLAR" 16570 W/ BOX & PAPERS SET Value: £7,000 Duration: 7 Days Age: Dated 2001 Max. Entries: 699 Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 07/07/26 The Rolex Explorer II is one of the great modern classic wristwatches , and this is an excellent example of the 16570 reference . Dating to 2001 , and known as the “Polar” for its attractive white dial, this example is in excellent, sharp condition. The watch comes with its original box, papers, booklets, tag and more, and was originally sold in Hong Kong. 20.89% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 146 699 20.89% 553 Tickets remaining 553/699 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 5 Days 17 Hours 55 Minutes 53 Seconds ENTER NOW WIN A ROLEX EXPLORER II "POLAR" 16570 BOX & PAPERS ENTER NOW 20.89% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 146 699 20.89% 553 Tickets remaining 553/699 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 5 Days 17 Hours 55 Minutes 53 Seconds Value: £7,000 Duration: 7 Days Age: Dated 2001 Entries: 699 Max. Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 07/07/2026 The Rolex Explorer II is one of the great modern classic wristwatches , and this is an excellent example of the 16570 reference . Dating to 2001 , and known as the “Polar” for its attractive white dial, this example is in excellent, sharp condition. The watch comes with its original box, papers, booklets, tag and more, and was originally sold in Hong Kong. #31 - The Rolex Explorer II "Polar" 16570 w/ Original Box & Papers Set 2001 The History— The Example The Rolex Explorer II evolved from the earlier Explorer models by introducing a fixed 24-hour bezel and additional GMT hand, transforming it from a simple time-only tool watch into a purpose-built companion for explorers working in environments where distinguishing day from night could prove difficult. The reference 16570 is widely regarded as the sweet spot of the Explorer II lineage, combining classic proportions with the reliable Rolex calibre 3185 movement and independently adjustable GMT hand. Dating to 2001 , this “Polar” example features the model’s iconic white dial, offering superb legibility and a distinctive look that has become increasingly popular with collectors. Presented in excellent, sharp condition , the watch has been exceptionally well preserved and is accompanied by its original box, papers, booklets, tag and accessories . Originally supplied in Hong Kong , it is a superb, complete example of one of Rolex’s most wearable and increasingly collectible sports watches. ENTER NOW The First Competition of its Kind The Vintage Watch Draw is unique. We’re the first competition website dedicated to collector-grade vintage watches, backed by a trusted expert. After the initial idea over five years ago, we’re so excited to now bring this new concept to you. Curated by A Trusted Expert Our competitions are curated by Alex Stevens, a trusted & reputable vintage watch specialist with lifelong experience in the trade. 12 Month Service Warranty Every watch is fully serviced by our watchmaker & comes with a 12 month mechanical warranty, so you can enjoy your watch hassle-free. Collector-Grade Condition Every watch is meticulously curated by Alex, known for selecting only the finest, collector-grade examples in terms of condition & originality. Low & Transparent Odds We don’t flood the draw with tickets. Our competitions are deliberately limited to keep odds low & your chance of winning fair. Authenticity Guarantee Every watch is thoroughly inspected & fully authenticated by our in-house expert. We are professionals, not enthusiasts or hobbyists. Guaranteed Draws Every draw ends on the stated date, no matter what. We never extend our competitions, even if we haven't sold all the tickets.
